UCS-23  MAXIMUM ALLOWABLE STRESS VALUES FOR CARBON AND LOW ALLOY STEELS. (a) The maximum allowable stress values for carbon and low alloy steels at various temperatures are given in the tables of this paragraph. (b) Stress values shall be used in the design formulas in accordance with UG-27 and UG-28. The stress value at the design temperature as required by UG-20 shall govern. (c) For materials at temperatures in the creep range, time-dependent stress values shall be used, except as permitted by this paragraph for short duration loading. See also UG-23 for general stress requirements. For high alloy steel stress values, refer to UHA-23. Material specifications shall comply with UG-80.
